skills.title: Superpowered Planning Skill
skills.summary: Plans, tests, searches for edge cases and catches gaps before execution starts.

source_lineage.notes: Inspired by external skill references; rebuilt as a Nexus canonical skill after research and improvement review.

when_to_use: before dispatching to Runner or Nexus
procedure: Restate objective, constraints and non-goals.
Split work into ordered steps with owners, outputs and gates.
Identify assumptions, missing inputs and hidden dependencies.
Generate edge cases, abuse cases and failure cases before execution.
Attach each edge case to a gate, eval hook or evidence requirement.
Define minimal test strategy and rollback or handoff path.
Produce a compact execution plan ready for task packet conversion.
edge_cases: tool or skill selected only because of name similarity
failure_modes: plan looks complete but cannot be executed
edge cases listed but not attached to gates
skill or tool chosen without vetting

research_questions: What planning failures occur most often in agentic execution?
Which edge-case discovery methods are most reliable?

improvement_notes: Add eval set for planning completeness.
Separate plan quality from execution quality.
Add skill-selection audit trace to generated task packet.

skills.title: Quality Assurance Skill
skills.summary: Turns acceptance criteria, regression risks and evidence requirements into a quality-control loop.

source_lineage.notes: Converted into Nexus QA skill with explicit evidence and gate requirements.

procedure: Map requirements to testable checks.
Classify checks as critical, regression-sensitive or advisory.
Identify happy-path, empty, loading, error and boundary states.
Request or generate evidence for each critical gate.
Flag missing evidence separately from failed evidence.
Assign evidence strength: direct test, manual proof, screenshot, log, assertion or unsupported.
Produce release recommendation with blockers and non-blockers separated.
edge_cases: passing test that does not cover requirement
failure_modes: mixing spec review with implementation quality

research_questions: Which evidence types best predict production readiness?
How should QA detect unsupported outputs?

improvement_notes: Add separate manual vs automated evidence classes.

skills.title: Execution Acceleration Skill
skills.summary: Converts a plan into minimal, concrete, unblockable execution steps with bias toward finishing useful work.

source_lineage.notes: Added as a distinct execution behavior not covered by normal planning or QA.

procedure: Identify the smallest useful deliverable.
Cut non-essential scope into a visible cut_scope_list.
Convert blockers into direct next actions with owners.
Prefer reversible progress over abstract discussion.
Preserve safety, evidence and rollback gates even while accelerating.
Define done and evidence for done.
Escalate unresolved blockers instead of hiding them.
failure_modes: cutting required scope without user-visible trace

research_questions: How can agents avoid over-planning while preserving safety?
What is the minimal evidence needed for done?

skills.title: Review Skill
skills.summary: Checks whether the output satisfies the requested spec, quality expectations and evidence requirements.

source_lineage.notes: Mapped into Nexus review and report flow.

procedure: Compare output to requirements and acceptance criteria.
Check missing scope and unrequested extra scope.
Map important claims to evidence.
Classify findings as blocker, major, minor or advisory.
Separate spec compliance from style and implementation quality.
Recommend release, rework or research follow-up.
failure_modes: failing to separate blocker and advisory issues

research_questions: What review criteria catch the most consequential errors?

improvement_notes: Add separate spec, style and security sections.

skills.title: Ultra Review Skill
skills.summary: Adversarial, multi-angle review for contradictions, hidden risks, missing evidence, edge cases and production readiness.

source_lineage.notes: Added as stronger review mode than standard review.

procedure: Run standard spec compliance review.
Run contradiction, assumption and stale-context scan.
Run edge-case and abuse-case scan.
Check evidence strength for all important claims.
Check tool/skill security, permissions and descriptor trust.
Check production readiness, rollback and observability.
Check memory/context side effects.
Return prioritized blockers and non-blockers.
failure_modes: security review that ignores tool metadata

research_questions: Which adversarial review steps reduce severe agent failures?
How can ultra review avoid excessive false positives?

skills.title: Context Mode Skill
skills.summary: Maintains the right working set, context budget and next-loop handoff without bloating the prompt or memory.

source_lineage.notes: Rebuilt around Nexus context update packets and working-set policies.

procedure: Identify current objective and next decision point.
Keep relevant constraints, active blockers and latest decisions.
Summarize large artifacts into evidence-linked summaries.
Detect conflicts between current context and memory.
Drop stale, irrelevant or duplicated context with a visible drop_list.
Emit next-loop context packet.
edge_cases: old decision conflicts with new decision
retrieved memory is similar but wrong
failure_modes: mixing durable memory with temporary context

research_questions: How should working sets be selected for agent execution?

skills.title: Frontend Skill
skills.summary: Builds UI with component structure, state coverage, responsive behavior, accessibility and API contract fit.

source_lineage.notes: Mapped to frontend-web-engineer and frontend-web-pack; now made canonical.

procedure: Map user flows and primary jobs-to-be-done.
Define component tree and data ownership.
Define loading, empty, error, partial and success states.
Check responsive behavior across target breakpoints.
Check keyboard and accessibility baseline.
Check API contract, validation and error handling.
Produce frontend handoff with testable state matrix.

research_questions: Which frontend failure modes most often break real user flows?
What baseline accessibility gates should be mandatory?

skills.title: Memory Skill
skills.summary: Decides what should become durable memory, what should be deprecated and what should stay temporary context.

source_lineage.notes: Mapped to Memory Curator and memory update packet; made canonical for pack references.

procedure: Classify durable decisions, preferences, constraints and lessons.
Separate temporary context from memory.
Check new memory against existing records for conflict or supersession.
Mark superseded assumptions and deprecated records.
Avoid storing raw logs, transient blockers or sensitive over-retained data.
Emit memory update packet with do_not_store list.
edge_cases: temporary blocker mistaken as durable preference
old memory conflicts with new decision

research_questions: What should agents store as durable memory?
How should stale or conflicting memory be resolved?
